--- a/tests/test_relational.py

         assert_array_equal(ax.collections[0].get_facecolors(), colors)
 
+    def test_hue_order(self, long_df):
+
+        order = categorical_order(long_df["a"])
+        unused = order.pop()
+
+        ax = scatterplot(data=long_df, x="x", y="y", hue="a", hue_order=order)
+        points = ax.collections[0]
+        assert (points.get_facecolors()[long_df["a"] == unused] == 0).all()
+        assert [t.get_text() for t in ax.legend_.texts] == order
+
     def test_linewidths(self, long_df):
 
         f, ax = plt.subplots()

problem_statement.Title: pairplot fails with hue_order not containing all hue values in seaborn 0.11.1
problem_statement.Description: When using seaborn pairplot with a hue_order parameter that contains only a subset of the unique values in the hue column, the plot fails with a TypeError about ufunc 'isnan' not supported. This worked in seaborn versions prior to 0.11.
RootCause: 1. In PairGrid.__init__ (axisgrid.py line 1281), hue_names and hue_order are correctly set to the user-provided subset, but at line 1286 self.hue_vals = data[hue] retains ALL hue values from the original data instead of filtering to match hue_order. 2. In pairplot function (axisgrid.py line 2108), plot_kws['style'] = data[hue] passes the entire hue column to scatterplot rather than using filtered data that matches hue_order. This mismatch causes numpy to call isnan() on string category data when the style parameter contains hue values not being iterated over.
StepsToReproduce: Import seaborn and load the iris dataset
Attempt to create a pairplot with hue='species' and hue_order containing only a subset of species: sns.pairplot(iris, hue='species', hue_order=['setosa', 'versicolor'])
problem_statement.ExpectedBehavior: The pairplot should display data only for the hue values specified in hue_order, ignoring other values in the hue column. Points with hue values not in the list should not be plotted.
problem_statement.ObservedBehavior: The pairplot raises a TypeError: ufunc 'isnan' not supported for the input types, and the inputs could not be safely coerced to any supported types according to the casting rule ''safe''.

hints_text:
The following workarounds seem to work:
```
g.map(sns.scatterplot, hue=iris["species"], hue_order=iris["species"].unique())
```
or
```
g.map(lambda x, y, **kwargs: sns.scatterplot(x=x, y=y, hue=iris["species"]))
```
> ```
> g.map(sns.scatterplot, hue=iris["species"], hue_order=iris["species"].unique())
> ```

The workaround fixes the problem for me.
Thank you very much!

@mwaskom Should I close the Issue or leave it open until the bug is fixed?
That's a good workaround, but it's still a bug. The problem is that `PairGrid` now lets `hue` at the grid-level delegate to the axes-level functions if they have `hue` in their signature. But it's not properly handling the case where `hue` is *not* set for the grid, but *is* specified for one mapped function. @jhncls's workaround suggests the fix.

An easier workaround would have been to set `PairGrid(..., hue="species")` and then pass `.map(..., hue=None)` where you don't want to separate by species. But `regplot` is the one axis-level function that does not yet handle hue-mapping internally, so it doesn't work for this specific case. It would have if you wanted a single bivariate density over hue-mapped scatterplot points (i.e. [this example](http://seaborn.pydata.org/introduction.html#classes-and-functions-for-making-complex-graphics) or something similar.

patch:
diff --git a/seaborn/_stats/regression.py b/seaborn/_stats/regression.py
--- a/seaborn/_stats/regression.py
+++ b/seaborn/_stats/regression.py
@@ -38,7 +38,10 @@ def _fit_predict(self, data):
 
     def __call__(self, data, groupby, orient, scales):
 
-        return groupby.apply(data, self._fit_predict)
+        return (
+            groupby
+            .apply(data.dropna(subset=["x", "y"]), self._fit_predict)
+        )
 
 
 @dataclass

+++ b/tests/_stats/test_regression.py

+from pandas.testing import assert_frame_equal
 
 from seaborn._core.groupby import GroupBy

             grid = np.linspace(part["x"].min(), part["x"].max(), gridsize)
             assert_array_equal(part["x"], grid)
             assert part["y"].diff().diff().dropna().abs().gt(0).all()
+
+    def test_missing_data(self, df):
+
+        groupby = GroupBy(["group"])
+        df.iloc[5:10] = np.nan
+        res1 = PolyFit()(df[["x", "y"]], groupby, "x", {})
+        res2 = PolyFit()(df[["x", "y"]].dropna(), groupby, "x", {})
+        assert_frame_equal(res1, res2)
\ No newline at end of file

problem_statement.Title: PolyFit fails with LinAlgError when data contains missing values
problem_statement.Description: The PolyFit statistical transform crashes with a LinAlgError when the input data contains None or NaN values. The np.polyfit() function cannot handle arrays with missing values.
problem_statement.RootCause: 1. The PolyFit._fit_predict method in seaborn/_stats/regression.py passes x and y arrays directly to np.polyfit() without filtering out NaN values, assuming the data is complete. 2. The method lacks a dropna call to handle missing data, unlike other statistical transforms in the codebase (e.g., aggregation.py uses data.dropna(subset=['x', 'y'])).
problem_statement.ExpectedBehavior: The plot should render successfully, skipping rows with missing x or y values.
problem_statement.ObservedBehavior: The plot fails with LinAlgError: SVD did not converge in Linear Least Squares

patch:
diff --git a/seaborn/_core/scales.py b/seaborn/_core/scales.py
--- a/seaborn/_core/scales.py
+++ b/seaborn/_core/scales.py
@@ -346,7 +346,7 @@ def _setup(
                 vmin, vmax = data.min(), data.max()
             else:
                 vmin, vmax = new.norm
-            vmin, vmax = axis.convert_units((vmin, vmax))
+            vmin, vmax = map(float, axis.convert_units((vmin, vmax)))
             a = forward(vmin)
             b = forward(vmax) - forward(vmin)

problem_statement.Title: Color mapping fails with boolean data
problem_statement.Description: When using boolean values for the color encoding in seaborn's Plot API, a TypeError is raised. Attempting to plot with `color=[True, False]` fails because the scale setup attempts arithmetic operations (subtraction) on boolean values, which NumPy does not support.
problem_statement.RootCause:
1. The `Color` property class in `seaborn/_core/properties.py` is missing a `default_scale` method. When no explicit scale is provided, the system calls `prop.default_scale(values)` which falls back to the base `Property` class (lines 62-76 in properties.py) that uses `boolean_type="numeric"`, incorrectly treating boolean data as numeric.
2. This causes a `Continuous` scale to be returned instead of `Nominal`.
3. When `Continuous._setup()` is called with boolean data at `seaborn/_core/scales.py:351`, it attempts `forward(vmax) - forward(vmin)` arithmetic on boolean values, which fails because NumPy boolean subtraction is unsupported.
4. The `Color.infer_scale` method (lines 567-607 in properties.py) correctly uses `variable_type(data, boolean_type="categorical")` to return `Nominal` for boolean data, but this logic is not present in the `default_scale` method which is used when no explicit scale/arg is provided.
StepsToReproduce: Import seaborn: import seaborn as so
Import seaborn's object-oriented plotting: from seaborn import Plot as soPlot
Create a plot with boolean color values: soPlot(['a', 'b'], [1, 2], color=[True, False]).add(so.Bar())
problem_statement.ExpectedBehavior: Boolean values in the color encoding should be treated as categorical data, with each unique boolean value (True, False) mapped to a distinct color, and the plot should render successfully.
problem_statement.ObservedBehavior: A TypeError is raised: 'numpy boolean subtract, the `-` operator, is not supported, use the bitwise_ xor, the `^` operator, or the logical_xor function instead.'

hints_text: Would this simply mean refactoring the code to use `^` or `xor` functions instead?

patch:
diff --git a/seaborn/axisgrid.py b/seaborn/axisgrid.py
--- a/seaborn/axisgrid.py
+++ b/seaborn/axisgrid.py
@@ -1472,8 +1472,8 @@ def map_diag(self, func, **kwargs):
                 for ax in diag_axes[1:]:
                     share_axis(diag_axes[0], ax, "y")
 
-            self.diag_vars = np.array(diag_vars, np.object_)
-            self.diag_axes = np.array(diag_axes, np.object_)
+            self.diag_vars = diag_vars
+            self.diag_axes = diag_axes
 
         if "hue" not in signature(func).parameters:
             return self._map_diag_iter_hue(func, **kwargs)

+++ b/tests/test_axisgrid.py

         with pytest.warns(UserWarning):
             g = ag.pairplot(self.df, hue="a", vars=vars, markers=markers[:-2])
 
+    def test_pairplot_column_multiindex(self):

+        cols = pd.MultiIndex.from_arrays([["x", "y"], [1, 2]])
+        df = self.df[["x", "y"]].set_axis(cols, axis=1)
+        g = ag.pairplot(df)
+        assert g.diag_vars == list(cols)
+
     def test_corner_despine(self):
 
         g = ag.PairGrid(self.df, corner=True, despine=False)

problem_statement.Title: pairplot raises KeyError with MultiIndex DataFrame
problem_statement.Description: When passing a DataFrame with MultiIndex columns (column names as tuples like ('A', '1'), ('A', '2'), etc.) to seaborn's pairplot function, a KeyError is raised. The error occurs in the map_diag method when attempting to access data columns, as the tuple column names become corrupted during numpy array processing.
problem_statement.RootCause: 1. In axisgrid.py line 1475 within the map_diag method, the code stores diag_vars as a numpy object array via `self.diag_vars = np.array(diag_vars, np.object_)`. When tuples from MultiIndex columns are stored in a numpy object array and then iterated, numpy treats them incorrectly—iterating over np.array([('A', '1'), ('A', '2')], np.object_) yields ('A',) (first element only) instead of the full tuple ('A', '1'). This corrupted tuple is then used at line 1490 to access self.data[var], causing the KeyError because ('A',) does not exist in the DataFrame columns. 2. In axisgrid.py lines 1659-1662 within the _add_axis_labels method, tuple labels passed to matplotlib's set_xlabel/set_ylabel do not render properly as axis labels.
StepsToReproduce: Create a dictionary with MultiIndex column tuples as keys
Populate it with random numpy arrays as values
Convert the dictionary to a pandas DataFrame
problem_statement.ExpectedBehavior: seaborn's pairplot should generate a grid of plots for all column pairs in the MultiIndex DataFrame, displaying proper axis labels for each subplot.
problem_statement.ObservedBehavior: pairplot raises KeyError: "['1'] not in index" when trying to access self.data[var] for a MultiIndex DataFrame. The error occurs because column name tuples become corrupted during numpy array processing.
